info.name: Lenovo AIBackend — US04 Data Hygiene
info.description:
End-to-end Postman collection for Sprint 1A · US04 (Data Hygiene, Validation & Intelligent Alerts).

Prereqs:
1. Run `sql/2026_06_us04_data_task.sql` against your dev Postgres.
2. Start AIBackend:  `uvicorn app.main:app --reload --port 8001`
3. (Optional, only for the /scan endpoint) Start D365 Sales backend on port 8000.

Variables (override in the collection's Variables tab if you want):
  baseUrl       — defaults to http://localhost:8001
  owner_id      — the seller UUID; used as the task assignee on every create
  entity_id_a   — first opportunity UUID (used by Steps 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 9)
  entity_id_b   — second opportunity UUID (used by Steps 6, 7, 8)
  actor_id      — UUID of whoever's resolving / dismissing
  task_a_id     — auto-populated by Step 1 (don't edit)
  task_b_id     — auto-populated by Step 6 (don't edit)

Run the requests in order — later steps depend on task_a_id / task_b_id
which are extracted into collection variables by earlier ones.

Test assertions live in each request's Tests tab. Use Collection Runner
(or Newman) to run the whole flow in one shot.

item.name: 01 · Create task A (transcript signal — close-date conflict)

exec: pm.test('was_existing is false on first create', () => {

body.raw:
{
  "owner_id": "{{owner_id}}",
  "entity_kind": "opportunity",
  "entity_id": "{{entity_id_a}}",
  "task_kind": "transcript_signal_close_date_different",
  "severity": "high",
  "confidence": "high",
  "field_name": "close_date",
  "current_value": "2026-06-30",
  "suggested_value": "2026-06-15",
  "evidence_ref": "transcript_segment_id=ab12cd34",
  "evidence_text": "Customer mentioned: We need it before June 15th on the call.",
  "created_by_source": "transcript"
}

request.description:
Caller in production: AI team (transcript-signal NLP pipeline).

Creates a fresh task. The combination of (entity_kind, entity_id, task_kind) becomes the idempotency key — re-posting the same payload returns the same task_id (see Step 02).

Saves the new task_id into the {{task_a_id}} collection variable so later steps can target it.

exec: pm.test('returns the SAME task_id from Step 01', () => {

body.raw:
{
  "owner_id": "{{owner_id}}",
  "entity_kind": "opportunity",
  "entity_id": "{{entity_id_a}}",
  "task_kind": "transcript_signal_close_date_different",
  "severity": "high",
  "confidence": "high",
  "field_name": "close_date",
  "current_value": "2026-06-30",
  "suggested_value": "2026-06-15",
  "evidence_ref": "transcript_segment_id=ab12cd34",
  "evidence_text": "Customer mentioned: We need it before June 15th on the call.",
  "created_by_source": "transcript"
}

request.description:
Idempotency contract: re-posting the same (entity_kind, entity_id, task_kind) returns the existing task with `was_existing: true`. No duplicate row is created.

This is what lets the AI team and the daily scan re-post safely.

item.name: 03 · List tasks for the seller (FE queue)

exec: pm.test('every item belongs to the requested owner', () => {

query.description: AC #11 portfolio scoping — FE always sends this.

request.description:
Caller: Frontend (To-Do List view).

Optional filters not shown above:
  kind        — filter by task_kind (e.g. past_close_date)
  entityKind  — opportunity / account / contact
  entityId    — show all tasks for one record (deal-detail page)
  offset      — pagination offset

Response items are ordered:
  confidence DESC NULLS LAST,
  severity   DESC,
  created_at ASC.

Caller: Frontend (when seller clicks into a task).

Returns the full DataTaskOut record. 404 on unknown UUID.

Caller: Frontend (when seller approves the suggestion / marks fixed).

Writes resolved_at, resolved_value, actor_id on the row. Idempotent — second resolve returns was_already_resolved: true and does NOT overwrite the original audit trail.

item.name: 06 · Create task B (different entity, to test dismiss flow)

body.raw:
{
  "owner_id": "{{owner_id}}",
  "entity_kind": "opportunity",
  "entity_id": "{{entity_id_b}}",
  "task_kind": "transcript_signal_budget_different",
  "severity": "medium",
  "confidence": "medium",
  "field_name": "estimated_value",
  "current_value": "195000",
  "suggested_value": "180000",
  "evidence_ref": "transcript_segment_id=ef56gh78",
  "evidence_text": "Customer said their approved budget is $180K, but the deal value in D365 shows $195K.",
  "created_by_source": "transcript"
}

request.description: Different entity_id_b + different task_kind → fresh row. Used by Step 07 (dismiss) and Step 08 (suppression check).

item.name: 07 · Dismiss task B with a note

request.description:
Caller: Frontend (when seller clicks Dismiss and types a reason).

AC #4: dismissals MUST come with a note. The DB has a CHECK constraint as a final guard against empty notes.

Dismissed tasks block future re-creation of the same (entity, kind) — that's the suppression check in Step 08.

exec: pm.test('the returned task is STILL DISMISSED (not regenerated)', () => {

body.raw:
{
  "owner_id": "{{owner_id}}",
  "entity_kind": "opportunity",
  "entity_id": "{{entity_id_b}}",
  "task_kind": "transcript_signal_budget_different",
  "severity": "medium",
  "confidence": "medium",
  "field_name": "estimated_value",
  "current_value": "195000",
  "suggested_value": "180000",
  "evidence_ref": "transcript_segment_id=ef56gh78",
  "evidence_text": "Customer said their approved budget is $180K, but the deal value in D365 shows $195K.",
  "created_by_source": "transcript"
}

request.description:
AC #5 acceptance criterion in action: "intentional mismatches can be dismissed with a note and future alerts for that pair are suppressed."

This is the same payload as Step 06. Because the matching task is currently dismissed, the system returns it untouched instead of creating a fresh open task. The seller's dismissal is honoured.

AC #3 grounding: "no alert is generated without a grounding reference."

Empty evidence_text fails Pydantic min_length=1 → 422 before the request even reaches the service layer.

AC #4 belt-and-braces enforcement: a whitespace-only note (3 chars, passes Pydantic min_length=1) is rejected by the service layer with 409.

Fully empty note (\"\") would 422 at the Pydantic layer instead.

item.name: 11 · Manual scan trigger (DRY-RUN — needs D365 Sales running)

exec: // This endpoint requires the D365 Sales backend to be reachable
// at D365_BASE_URL (default http://localhost:8000). If it's not
// running, the response will be a 500 — which is also a valid
// signal that you need to start D365 Sales first.
pm.test('500 likely means D365 Sales is not running — start it on port 8000 and retry', () => {

request.description:
Caller in production: cron (via the CLI: `python -m app.jobs.scan_data_tasks`). This HTTP version is for ad-hoc / admin runs.

PRECONDITION: the Lenovo D365 Sales backend MUST be running on the URL set by D365_BASE_URL (default http://localhost:8000). The scan calls D365 over HTTP to enumerate active opportunities and fetch per-deal risks.

With dry_run=true: detectors run, no rows are written, the summary shows what WOULD have been created.
With limit=5: scan stops after 5 opps — keeps the test cheap.

Flip dry_run to false and remove limit for a real scan.
